expectations.evidence: No malloc, new, std::vector, or dynamic allocation anywhere. Uses xTaskCreateStatic instead of xTaskCreate, configSUPPORT_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION set to 0, and all buffers are static arrays.

All buffers are statically allocated: adc_dma_buf[32] is static with __attribute__((aligned(4))), adc_work_buf[16] is static, adc_task_stack and adc_task_tcb are static. Memory budget table confirms all in .bss (static RAM).

Uses volatile pointer (static volatile uint16_t *ready_half_ptr) for ISR-to-task data passing, vTaskNotifyGiveFromISR/ulTaskNotifyTake for signaling, portYIELD_FROM_ISR for context switch, and correct NVIC priority configuration relative to configLIBRARY_MAX_SYSCALL_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY.

Uses __attribute__((aligned(4))) for the DMA buffer and explicitly states 'STM32F4 (Cortex-M4) has no D-cache, so alignas(4) is sufficient for DMA buffers. No cache maintenance needed.' No over-specification to 32-byte alignment.

The response uses a double-buffer (ping-pong) DMA scheme rather than a ring buffer with index masking. ADC_BURST_SIZE is 16 (power of 2) but there is no ring buffer with bitmask indexing. The design uses pointer swapping between buffer halves instead of modulo or bitmask indexing.
